task_groups.title: Foundations and repository scaffolding
task_groups.description: Core repo setup, workspace structure, and project baseline. Mostly complete.

task_groups.title: Workflow model and schemas
task_groups.description: Rust types for WorkflowDefinition, NodeDefinition, RunInstance, and related structs. Core structs exist; RunInstance and typed node configs are missing.

task_groups.title: Event system and typed events
task_groups.description: Typed event families for replay, monitoring, and audit. Envelope exists; all typed event modules are placeholders.

task_groups.title: Core execution engine
task_groups.description: State machines, scheduler, validation, execution coordinator, and guardrails. All placeholders.

task_groups.title: SQLite persistence layer
task_groups.description: Database schema, migrations, and repository access. All placeholders.

task_groups.title: Runtime adapters
task_groups.description: CLI shell adapter and agent CLI adapter for executing commands in a workspace. All placeholders.

task_groups.title: Tauri desktop shell and bridge
task_groups.description: Tauri command handlers and event bridge wiring the Rust engine to the React frontend. All stubs.

task_groups.title: Builder view
task_groups.description: React/TypeScript workflow builder canvas, node palette, property inspector, and save/load. Not started.

task_groups.title: Live run monitor view
task_groups.description: Animated graph state, event feed, command output panel, and human review interaction. Not started.

task_groups.title: Replay and debugging view
task_groups.description: Timeline scrubber, event inspector, graph playback, and diff panes. Not started.

task_groups.title: UX, library view, and distribution
task_groups.description: Library view, global keyboard navigation, visual theme, first-run experience, and distribution artifacts.

task_groups.title: Documentation upkeep
task_groups.description: Keeping docs in sync with implementation as the system is built.

task_groups.title: Testing
task_groups.description: Rust unit tests, frontend component tests, and E2E tests via tauri-driver + WebdriverIO.

task_groups.title: Unresolved decisions
task_groups.description: Open design decisions that must be resolved before or during implementation of their affected areas.

tasks.title: Rust workspace and crate skeleton
tasks.summary: Cargo workspace with all crates created and building.
tasks.description: The root Cargo.toml workspace is configured with crates: workflow-model, event-model, core-engine, runtime-adapters, persistence, simulation, and apps/desktop/src-tauri. Shared workspace dependencies are declared.
acceptance_criteria: cargo build succeeds from workspace root
All documented crates exist and have Cargo.toml
Workspace resolver = 2
notes: Done. Verified via Cargo.toml and directory layout.

tasks.title: Frontend project setup
tasks.summary: Vite + React + TypeScript + React Flow + Vitest frontend project configured.
tasks.description: apps/desktop has package.json with React 18, TypeScript 5, Vite 5, Vitest, React Testing Library, reactflow, and Tauri API packages. Tauri mock infrastructure for tests is in place.
acceptance_criteria: npm install succeeds
npm run dev starts the dev server
npm test runs Vitest
Tauri API mock exists at src/__mocks__/@tauri-apps/api/core.ts
notes: Done. reactflow ^11 included. Tauri API mock in place.

tasks.title: JSON schema files for workflow and run-event
tasks.summary: Draft JSON Schema documents for WorkflowDefinition and RunEvent exist.
tasks.description: schemas/workflow.schema.json and schemas/run-event.schema.json have been created with draft-07 JSON Schema definitions covering the core fields from the architecture docs.
acceptance_criteria: schemas/workflow.schema.json validates against a well-formed workflow JSON
schemas/run-event.schema.json covers the RunEvent envelope fields
notes: Schema exists but may need updating as typed event payloads are finalized.

tasks.title: Canonical demo workflow JSON
tasks.summary: Plan -> Execute Tool -> Critique -> Approve workflow.json exists with all 6 nodes and 5 edges.
tasks.description: examples/plan-execute-critique-approve/workflow.json is populated with Start, Agent(Plan), Tool(Execute), Agent(Critique), HumanReview(Approve), End nodes and appropriate edges and memory access contracts.
acceptance_criteria: workflow.json is valid against schemas/workflow.schema.json
All six node types present in the file match the v1 node taxonomy
All edges use correct condition_kind values
notes: Fully populated. Used as the integration test target for all test layers.

tasks.title: CI pipeline setup
tasks.summary: GitHub Actions pipeline running cargo test, npm test, and (on PR) E2E tests.
tasks.description: Create a .github/workflows/ci.yml (or equivalent) that runs: (1) cargo test for all Rust crates, (2) npm test for the frontend, (3) E2E tests against a Tauri debug build on PR/pre-merge. Use Xvfb on Linux for E2E. Cache cargo and node_modules.
acceptance_criteria: CI runs on push and PR
cargo test stage passes
npm test stage passes
E2E stage runs tauri-driver + WebdriverIO against a compiled binary
Pipeline clearly separates fast (unit) and slow (E2E) stages
notes: E2E requires a headful environment or Xvfb. Consider running E2E only on PRs to main.

tasks.title: WorkflowDefinition, NodeDefinition, EdgeDefinition structs
tasks.summary: Core workflow model types are implemented in crates/workflow-model.
tasks.description: WorkflowDefinition, NodeDefinition, NodeKind enum, EdgeDefinition, ConditionKind enum are implemented with serde Serialize/Deserialize. RetryPolicy and NodeDisplay are also implemented.
acceptance_criteria: All structs serialize to and deserialize from JSON correctly
NodeKind covers all 7 v1 node types
ConditionKind covers always, on_success, on_failure, expression
notes: Done. Input/output contracts stored as serde_json::Value — may need typed structs later per DEC-005.

tasks.title: MemoryState struct
tasks.summary: MemoryState with RunShared/NodeLocal scopes is implemented.
tasks.description: crates/workflow-model/src/memory.rs has MemoryScope enum and MemoryState struct with run_id, node_id, scope, and data fields.
acceptance_criteria: MemoryScope has RunShared and NodeLocal variants
MemoryState serializes/deserializes correctly
notes: Done.

tasks.title: RunInstance struct
tasks.summary: Implement the RunInstance struct representing one execution of a workflow.
tasks.description: Add RunInstance to crates/workflow-model or crates/core-engine with fields: run_id, workflow_id, workflow_version, status (RunStatus enum), workspace_root, created_at, started_at, ended_at, active_nodes, constraints, summary. Implement RunStatus enum with all lifecycle variants from ARCHITECTURE.md §7.2.
acceptance_criteria: RunInstance serializes/deserializes to JSON
RunStatus enum includes: Created, Validating, Ready, Running, Paused, Succeeded, Failed, Cancelled
RunInstance can be constructed from a WorkflowDefinition reference

tasks.title: NodeStatus enum and NodeSnapshot
tasks.summary: Implement NodeStatus lifecycle enum and NodeSnapshot for tracking per-node run state.
tasks.description: Add NodeStatus enum (Draft, Validated, Ready, Queued, Running, Waiting, Succeeded, Failed, Cancelled, Skipped) and NodeSnapshot struct (node_id, status, attempt, started_at, ended_at, output) to the model crate. These are needed by the state machine and persistence layers.
acceptance_criteria: NodeStatus has all 10 lifecycle variants from ARCHITECTURE.md §7.3
NodeSnapshot serializes correctly
NodeSnapshot can be stored and retrieved from persistence

tasks.title: Typed node config structs per NodeKind
tasks.summary: Replace generic serde_json::Value node config with typed structs per node kind.
tasks.description: Currently NodeDefinition.config is a serde_json::Value. Define typed config structs: AgentNodeConfig (prompt, provider hint), ToolNodeConfig (command, shell, timeout_ms), RouterNodeConfig (routing rules), MemoryNodeConfig (key, scope, operation), HumanReviewNodeConfig (reason, available_actions). Start and End nodes have no config. Use a tagged enum NodeConfig wrapping these.
acceptance_criteria: Each node kind has a typed config struct
NodeDefinition.config is a typed NodeConfig enum or struct
Existing example workflow.json still deserializes correctly after the change
Config structs are documented with their required fields
notes: Blocked on DEC-005 (structured output requirements for agent CLI nodes).

tasks.title: Guardrails/constraints model
tasks.summary: Implement a typed RunConstraints struct covering max_retries, max_runtime_ms, max_steps, and optional budget controls.
tasks.description: Replace serde_json::Value default_constraints in WorkflowDefinition with a typed RunConstraints struct. Include: max_retries (u32), max_runtime_ms (Option<u64>), max_steps (Option<u32>), optional budget fields. These constraints must be enforceable by the engine's guardrail system.
acceptance_criteria: RunConstraints struct is typed with documented fields
WorkflowDefinition.default_constraints uses RunConstraints
Example workflow.json still deserializes with the typed struct
Engine can read and enforce these values

tasks.title: Workflow model unit tests
tasks.summary: Unit tests for workflow-model: construction, serialization, and edge cases.
tasks.description: Write unit tests in crates/workflow-model/src/tests/ covering: WorkflowDefinition round-trip serialization, NodeKind variants, EdgeDefinition conditions, MemoryScope, RunInstance/NodeSnapshot construction, RunConstraints defaults.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p workflow-model passes
Round-trip JSON serialization is tested for all major types
Edge and boundary cases for enums are covered

tasks.title: RunEvent envelope
tasks.summary: Base RunEvent envelope struct with all required envelope fields is implemented.
tasks.description: crates/event-model/src/event.rs has RunEvent with event_id, run_id, workflow_id, node_id (Option), event_type (String), timestamp, payload (serde_json::Value), causation_id (Option), correlation_id (Option).
acceptance_criteria: RunEvent serializes to JSON matching the shape in EVENT_SCHEMA.md §2
All optional fields are correctly typed as Option
notes: Done. event_type is currently a String; consider typed enum in EVENT-002.

tasks.title: Run lifecycle event types
tasks.summary: Implement typed Rust structs/enums for all run lifecycle events.
tasks.description: Populate crates/event-model/src/run_events.rs with typed payload structs and a RunEventKind enum for: run.created, run.validation_started, run.validation_passed, run.validation_failed, run.started, run.paused, run.resumed, run.succeeded, run.failed, run.cancelled. Each variant should carry the relevant payload fields. Integrate with RunEvent by replacing the event_type String with or mapping to the typed enum.
acceptance_criteria: All 10 run lifecycle event types are represented
Payload structs have documented fields matching EVENT_SCHEMA.md
Events serialize/deserialize correctly
RunEvent can carry a typed payload variant

tasks.title: Node lifecycle event types
tasks.summary: Implement typed payload structs for all node lifecycle events.
tasks.description: Populate crates/event-model/src/node_events.rs with payload structs for: node.queued, node.started, node.waiting, node.succeeded, node.failed, node.cancelled, node.skipped, node.retry_scheduled. Include fields shown in EVENT_SCHEMA.md §4.1 (node_type, attempt, input_refs, workspace_root).
acceptance_criteria: All 8 node lifecycle event types are implemented
node.started payload includes node_type, attempt, input_refs, workspace_root
Events serialize/deserialize correctly

tasks.title: Command/tool execution event types
tasks.summary: Implement typed payload structs for command execution events.
tasks.description: Populate crates/event-model/src/command_events.rs with payload structs for: command.prepared, command.started, command.stdout, command.stderr, command.completed, command.failed. Payloads must include command string, shell, cwd, timeout_ms, exit_code, duration_ms, stdout/stderr bytes per EVENT_SCHEMA.md §4.2 and §4.3.
acceptance_criteria: All 6 command event types are implemented
command.started payload includes command, shell, cwd, timeout_ms
command.completed payload includes exit_code, duration_ms, stdout_bytes, stderr_bytes
Events serialize/deserialize correctly

tasks.title: Routing event types
tasks.summary: Implement typed payload structs for routing events.
tasks.description: Populate crates/event-model/src/routing_events.rs with payload structs for: edge.routed, router.evaluated, router.branch_selected, router.no_match. branch_selected payload must include router_node_id, selected_edge_ids, reason per EVENT_SCHEMA.md §4.4.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 routing event types are implemented
router.branch_selected payload matches EVENT_SCHEMA.md §4.4
Events serialize/deserialize correctly

tasks.title: Memory and agent event types
tasks.summary: Implement typed payload structs for memory events and agent interaction events.
tasks.description: Populate crates/event-model/src/memory_events.rs with: memory.read, memory.write, memory.snapshot_created. Implement agent interaction events (either in a new file or memory_events.rs): agent.request_prepared, agent.started, agent.output_received, agent.completed, agent.failed.
acceptance_criteria: All 3 memory event types are implemented
All 5 agent event types are implemented
Events serialize/deserialize correctly

tasks.title: Human review and guardrail event types
tasks.summary: Implement typed payload structs for human review events and guardrail/budget events.
tasks.description: Populate crates/event-model/src/human_review_events.rs with: review.required, review.approved, review.rejected, review.edited, review.retry_requested. review.required payload must include reason, blocking, available_actions per EVENT_SCHEMA.md §4.5. Add guardrail events: guardrail.warning, guardrail.exceeded, budget.updated.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 review event types are implemented
review.required payload matches EVENT_SCHEMA.md §4.5
All 3 guardrail event types are implemented
Events serialize/deserialize correctly

tasks.title: Event model unit tests
tasks.summary: Unit tests for event-model: serialization, deserialization, and field contracts.
tasks.description: Write tests in crates/event-model/src/tests/ covering round-trip JSON serialization for all event families. Verify that required envelope fields (event_id, run_id, workflow_id, timestamp) are always present. Verify optional fields are correctly handled as Option.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p event-model passes
Every event family has at least one round-trip test
No lossy transformation is permitted

tasks.title: Run state machine
tasks.summary: Implement the run lifecycle state machine with explicit, typed transitions.
tasks.description: Implement in crates/core-engine/src/state_machine/. Model RunStatus as a finite state machine. Define all valid transitions (e.g. Created -> Validating -> Ready -> Running -> Succeeded/Failed/Cancelled; Running -> Paused -> Running). Emit a typed RunEvent on every transition. Reject invalid transitions with a typed error. Do not infer state from timers or logs.
acceptance_criteria: All valid RunStatus transitions are implemented and tested
Invalid transitions return a typed error
Every transition emits the correct RunEvent type
State machine is deterministic and side-effect-free (pure transition logic)
notes: This is the foundation of the engine. Test every transition exhaustively.

tasks.title: Node state machine
tasks.summary: Implement per-node lifecycle state machine with typed transitions.
tasks.description: Implement in crates/core-engine/src/state_machine/. Model NodeStatus as a finite state machine per node. Define all valid transitions (e.g. Ready -> Queued -> Running -> Succeeded/Failed; Running -> Waiting for human review; Failed -> Queued on retry). Emit the correct typed NodeEvent on every transition.
acceptance_criteria: All valid NodeStatus transitions are implemented and tested
Invalid transitions return a typed error
Every transition emits the correct event type
Retry transitions increment attempt count correctly

tasks.title: Workflow validator
tasks.summary: Implement pre-run workflow validation that catches structural problems before execution begins.
tasks.description: Implement in crates/core-engine/src/validation/. Validate: (1) exactly one Start node, (2) at least one End node, (3) no orphaned nodes, (4) no cycles that would prevent termination (basic DAG check), (5) all edge source/target node IDs exist in the workflow, (6) required fields are present per NodeKind, (7) RunConstraints values are within sensible bounds. Return a structured ValidationResult with typed errors.
acceptance_criteria: Valid workflows pass validation
Each listed error condition is detected and returns a typed error
ValidationResult can be serialized and returned to the frontend for display
cargo test -p core-engine validation passes

tasks.title: Run coordinator and scheduler
tasks.summary: Implement the run coordinator that orchestrates node scheduling and execution sequencing.
tasks.description: Implement in crates/core-engine/src/scheduler/ and crates/core-engine/src/execution/. The coordinator should: (1) construct a run plan from a validated WorkflowDefinition, (2) queue nodes when their predecessors succeed, (3) invoke runtime adapters per node kind, (4) apply routing decisions from Router nodes, (5) handle retries per RetryPolicy, (6) pause execution at HumanReview nodes, (7) enforce guardrails. Use tokio for async execution. All state transitions must go through the state machine.
acceptance_criteria: A complete workflow runs from Start to End with all nodes transitioning through correct states
Node execution order matches the graph topology
Retry logic increments attempt and re-queues correctly
Guardrails halt the run when limits are exceeded
Events are emitted for every transition
notes: This is the most complex task in the project. Consider stubbing adapter calls for initial integration tests.

tasks.title: Router node evaluator
tasks.summary: Implement routing logic that evaluates ConditionKind rules and selects outgoing edges.
tasks.description: The engine must evaluate edges with condition_kind always, on_success, on_failure, and expression. For expression conditions, define a safe evaluation strategy (e.g. simple key-value matching against node output or memory — not an arbitrary script interpreter). Emit router.evaluated and router.branch_selected events. Handle router.no_match as a run failure.
acceptance_criteria: always edges are always followed
on_success and on_failure edges follow the node's terminal status
expression edges evaluate correctly against a defined rule format
router.no_match causes the run to fail with a clear error
router.branch_selected event is emitted with reason
notes: Keep expression evaluation minimal and safe in v1. Do not build a full expression language.

tasks.title: Human review gate handler
tasks.summary: Implement the engine-side handler that pauses a run at HumanReview nodes and resumes on decision.
tasks.description: Implement in crates/core-engine/src/review/. When a HumanReview node is reached: (1) emit review.required event with blocking=true, (2) transition node to Waiting state, (3) transition run to Paused state, (4) wait for an external signal (approve/reject/retry/edit_memory), (5) on receipt, apply the decision, emit the correct review event, and resume/fail/retry accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: Run correctly pauses at HumanReview nodes
review.required event is emitted
Approve resumes the run and transitions node to Succeeded
Reject transitions the run to Failed
Retry re-queues the relevant node
All decisions emit the correct review event

tasks.title: Guardrail enforcement
tasks.summary: Implement guardrail checks enforcing max_retries, max_runtime_ms, and max_steps.
tasks.description: During run execution, check guardrail limits at each node transition: (1) max_retries per node, (2) total run runtime against max_runtime_ms, (3) total steps against max_steps. Emit guardrail.warning when approaching limits and guardrail.exceeded when a limit is hit. On exceeded, transition the run to Failed with a clear reason.
acceptance_criteria: Runs exceeding max_steps are halted with guardrail.exceeded event
Runs exceeding max_runtime_ms are halted
Nodes exceeding max_retries are marked Failed
guardrail.warning is emitted before guardrail.exceeded

tasks.title: Core engine unit tests
tasks.summary: Comprehensive unit tests for state machines, validation, scheduler, and guardrails.
tasks.description: Write tests in crates/core-engine/src/tests/ covering: (1) every valid and invalid RunStatus transition, (2) every valid and invalid NodeStatus transition, (3) all validation failure scenarios, (4) scheduler sequencing with mock adapters, (5) routing rules for all ConditionKind variants, (6) human review pause/resume/reject, (7) guardrail enforcement. Use mock adapters — do not spawn real processes.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p core-engine passes
State machine transitions tested exhaustively (valid and invalid)
Routing rules tested for all ConditionKind variants
Human review flow tested
Guardrail limits tested

tasks.title: SQLite connection setup and schema migrations
tasks.summary: Initialize SQLite connection, run schema migrations on startup, and establish tables.
tasks.description: Implement in crates/persistence/src/sqlite/. Use the rusqlite or sqlx crate. On first launch, create tables: workflows, workflow_versions, runs, events, settings, and optionally artifacts. Migrations should be versioned (e.g. embedded via include_str! or a migrations directory). Provide a Db struct or connection pool abstraction used by all repositories.
acceptance_criteria: Application starts and creates the SQLite database at a configured path
All required tables are created on first launch
Migrations run in order and are idempotent
A Db connection type is accessible to all repository modules
notes: Use in-memory SQLite (:memory:) for all unit tests — never hit a real file in tests.

tasks.title: Workflow and version repository
tasks.summary: Implement CRUD repository for workflow definitions and versioned workflow metadata.
tasks.description: Implement in crates/persistence/src/repositories/. Provide functions: save_workflow, get_workflow_by_id, list_workflows, save_workflow_version, get_workflow_version. Store workflow JSON in a blob column. Support listing by recency and filtering by name.
acceptance_criteria: save_workflow persists a WorkflowDefinition
get_workflow_by_id retrieves it correctly
list_workflows returns all stored workflows
Versioning correctly tracks multiple versions of the same workflow_id
Unit tests pass with in-memory SQLite

tasks.title: Run repository
tasks.summary: Implement repository for RunInstance storage, retrieval, and status updates.
tasks.description: Provide functions: create_run, update_run_status, get_run_by_id, list_runs_for_workflow, get_active_runs. Store RunInstance and NodeSnapshot state. Support efficient lookup by run_id and workflow_id for the Library and Live Run views.
acceptance_criteria: create_run stores a RunInstance
update_run_status correctly updates the status field
list_runs_for_workflow returns all runs for a workflow_id ordered by recency
Unit tests pass with in-memory SQLite

tasks.title: Append-only event log repository
tasks.summary: Implement an append-only event log repository supporting replay and timeline queries.
tasks.description: The event log is the foundation of replay. Provide: append_event (insert-only, never update), list_events_for_run (ordered by timestamp), get_event_by_id, list_events_for_node. Store the full RunEvent JSON payload. Support pagination for large runs. Never allow deletion or mutation of stored events.
acceptance_criteria: append_event inserts an event and never updates existing rows
list_events_for_run returns all events in timestamp order
Replay can reconstruct node states from stored events for the demo workflow
Unit tests pass with in-memory SQLite
notes: This is append-only by design. Do not add update or delete functions for event rows.

tasks.title: Settings repository
tasks.summary: Implement a key-value settings repository for user preferences and workspace configuration.
tasks.description: Provide a simple settings table (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value JSON). Expose get_setting, set_setting, and list_settings functions. Store items such as default workspace path and UI preferences.
acceptance_criteria: get_setting and set_setting work correctly
Settings persist across app restarts

tasks.title: Persistence unit tests
tasks.summary: Unit tests for all persistence repositories using in-memory SQLite.
tasks.description: Write tests in crates/persistence/src/tests/ using an in-memory SQLite database. Cover: workflow CRUD, run CRUD, event append and query, settings read/write. Never hit a real file in tests.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p persistence passes
All repository functions have test coverage
Tests use :memory: SQLite exclusively

tasks.title: Adapter trait definition
tasks.summary: Define a common async Adapter trait that all runtime adapters implement.
tasks.description: Implement in crates/runtime-adapters/src/lib.rs. Define an async Adapter trait with methods: prepare, execute, and abort. The execute method receives a NodeDefinition, workspace_root, and run-shared memory; it returns a stream or structured output and emits events. Adapters must not mutate global state outside the workspace.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter trait is defined with prepare, execute, and abort methods
The trait is async (tokio-compatible)
A mock adapter implementing the trait exists for use in engine tests
Trait is documented with expected behavior per method

tasks.title: CLI shell command adapter
tasks.summary: Implement the CLI shell adapter that executes arbitrary shell commands in a workspace.
tasks.description: Implement in crates/runtime-adapters/src/cli/. Use tokio::process::Command to spawn processes inside workspace_root. Stream stdout and stderr, capturing them into command.stdout and command.stderr events. Record: command string, shell, cwd, exit code, duration_ms, stdout_bytes, stderr_bytes. Emit command.prepared, command.started, stdout/stderr events, and command.completed/command.failed. Support a configurable timeout (max_runtime_ms). Do not sanitize commands in v1 — all shell commands are allowed per DECISIONS.md.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter executes a shell command in the given workspace_root
stdout and stderr are captured and emitted as events
Exit code is correctly captured and returned
Timeout causes command.failed with a clear reason
All required metadata fields are logged
notes: Arbitrary shell commands are approved for v1. Log everything. Prefer patch-aware flows but support raw writes.

tasks.title: Agent CLI adapter
tasks.summary: Implement an agent CLI adapter that wraps external agent CLIs (e.g. claude-code, aider) as Agent node executors.
tasks.description: Implement in crates/runtime-adapters/src/cli/ or a new agent.rs. The adapter takes an Agent node config (prompt, provider hint) and executes an agent CLI command in the workspace. Capture structured or raw output. Emit agent.request_prepared, agent.started, agent.output_received, agent.completed/failed events. The command to run is configurable per node config.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter runs an agent CLI command with the given prompt/config
Agent interaction events are emitted
Output is captured and available for downstream nodes via memory
Failure (non-zero exit) emits agent.failed correctly
notes: Blocked on DEC-005: how much structured output is required from agent CLI nodes.

tasks.title: Adapter unit tests with mock execution
tasks.summary: Unit tests for runtime adapters using mock command execution — no real processes in tests.
tasks.description: Write tests in crates/runtime-adapters/src/tests/. Test: CLI adapter with a mocked process (e.g. echo command or in-process mock), event emission order and correctness, timeout behavior, stderr capture, non-zero exit code handling.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p runtime-adapters passes
No real agent CLIs or network calls in tests
Event emission order matches the documented flow
Timeout test verifies command.failed with the correct reason

tasks.title: Tauri commands for workflow CRUD
tasks.summary: Implement Tauri command handlers for creating, reading, updating, listing, and deleting workflows.
tasks.description: Implement in apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/. Add Tauri #[command] functions: create_workflow, get_workflow, list_workflows, update_workflow, delete_workflow, import_workflow, export_workflow. Delegate to the persistence layer — no execution logic here. Return serialized WorkflowDefinition or error to the frontend via invoke().
acceptance_criteria: All CRUD commands are registered with Tauri
Frontend can invoke each command via invoke()
Commands delegate to persistence — no engine logic in command handlers
Errors are returned as serializable error types

tasks.title: Tauri commands for run lifecycle
tasks.summary: Implement Tauri command handlers for creating, starting, pausing, resuming, and cancelling runs.
tasks.description: Add commands: create_run, start_run, pause_run, resume_run, cancel_run, get_run, list_runs. start_run initiates engine execution asynchronously — the engine emits events to the frontend via the event bridge. Commands must not block the frontend.
acceptance_criteria: All run lifecycle commands are registered with Tauri
start_run launches engine execution without blocking the invoke() call
cancel_run correctly signals the engine to stop
get_run returns current RunInstance state

tasks.title: Tauri commands for human review decisions
tasks.summary: Implement Tauri commands for approve, reject, and retry decisions on HumanReview nodes.
tasks.description: Add commands: submit_review_decision(run_id, node_id, decision: approve|reject|retry|edit_memory). The command signals the engine's human review gate handler to resume execution. Return the updated run state to the frontend.
acceptance_criteria: submit_review_decision command is registered
Approve signals the engine to resume the run
Reject signals the engine to fail the run
Retry signals the engine to re-queue the predecessor node
Correct review events are emitted

tasks.title: Tauri event bridge
tasks.summary: Implement the event bridge that emits engine RunEvents to the frontend via Tauri's app.emit().
tasks.description: Implement in apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/bridge/. The engine emits typed RunEvents to an internal event bus. The bridge subscribes to this bus and re-emits events to the frontend using app.emit() or window.emit(). The frontend subscribes using @tauri-apps/api listen(). The UI must not poll — it must receive pushed events.
acceptance_criteria: Engine events are forwarded to the frontend as Tauri events
Frontend receives events via listen(), not polling
All event families are forwarded (run, node, command, routing, memory, review, guardrail)
The bridge is thin — no state derivation or mutation
notes: Rule A: engine owns state. The bridge only forwards events. The UI renders from events.

tasks.title: Settings and workspace commands
tasks.summary: Tauri commands for reading/writing settings and selecting the workspace root directory.
tasks.description: Add commands: get_settings, set_setting, open_workspace_picker (triggers native OS file dialog to select a directory). Store the selected workspace_root in settings for use by run commands. Return the selected path to the frontend.
acceptance_criteria: open_workspace_picker opens the OS directory picker
Selected path is returned to the frontend and stored in settings
get_settings and set_setting work correctly

tasks.title: App shell and view router
tasks.summary: Implement the top-level React app shell with routing between Builder, LiveRun, Replay, and Library views.
tasks.description: Replace the App.tsx skeleton with a working view router. Implement a top-level nav bar or sidebar for switching views. Use React state or a router library (react-router or a simple custom router). Each view renders a placeholder initially. Keyboard shortcuts for view switching should be wired (per DESIGN_SPEC.md §13).
acceptance_criteria: Four views are reachable: Builder, LiveRun, Replay, Library
View switches without a full page reload
Keyboard shortcuts switch between views
App shell has the mission-control visual identity baseline

tasks.title: React Flow canvas and node rendering
tasks.summary: Implement the workflow builder canvas using React Flow with custom node components for all 7 node types.
tasks.description: Set up the React Flow canvas in the Builder view. Implement custom node components for: Start, End, Agent, Tool, Router, Memory, HumanReview. Each node shows an icon/type marker, label, and state badge. Nodes should be draggable and selectable. Initial load should support empty canvas and loading from a WorkflowDefinition.
acceptance_criteria: React Flow canvas renders all 7 custom node types
Nodes are draggable
Nodes are selectable with visual feedback
Canvas can load a WorkflowDefinition from JSON and render its nodes/edges
Node positions use NodeDefinition.display.x/y
notes: reactflow ^11 is already in package.json.

tasks.title: Node palette panel
tasks.summary: Implement a node palette panel for dragging new nodes onto the canvas.
tasks.description: Implement a sidebar panel listing all 7 node types with icons. Support drag-and-drop onto the canvas to create a new NodeDefinition with default config. Clicking should also add the node to a default position. Integrate with the React Flow instance.
acceptance_criteria: All 7 node types appear in the palette
Dragging a node type onto the canvas creates a new node
New nodes appear at the drop position
Newly created node gets a new UUID

tasks.title: Node property inspector panel
tasks.summary: Implement an inspector panel that shows and edits the selected node's configuration.
tasks.description: When a node is selected on the canvas, show a panel with editable fields for: label, config fields (per NodeKind), retry_policy, and input/output contracts. Changes update the in-memory WorkflowDefinition. Each node type should show relevant config fields.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting a node opens the inspector panel
Inspector shows appropriate config fields per NodeKind
Editing a field updates the WorkflowDefinition in state
Inspector updates when a different node is selected

tasks.title: Edge creation and validation feedback
tasks.summary: Enable edge creation between nodes and surface validation errors visually.
tasks.description: Allow users to draw edges between node ports. Edge creation should let the user pick a condition_kind (always, on_success, on_failure, expression). Wire up the validate_workflow Tauri command and display validation errors returned by the engine in a panel or inline on the canvas (e.g. invalid node highlighted in red, error tooltip).
acceptance_criteria: Edges can be drawn between nodes
Edge condition_kind is selectable during creation
Validation errors from the engine are displayed clearly
Invalid nodes or edges are visually flagged on the canvas
Validation runs before execution is allowed

tasks.title: Save and load workflow
tasks.summary: Wire save and load workflow actions to Tauri CRUD commands.
tasks.description: Implement Save (invoke create_workflow or update_workflow) and Load (invoke get_workflow or list_workflows then load). Show a workflow picker for loading. Serialize the current React Flow graph state to a WorkflowDefinition before saving. Deserialize on load and update the canvas.
acceptance_criteria: Save persists the current workflow via Tauri command
Load retrieves and renders a saved workflow on the canvas
Layout (node x/y positions) is preserved across save/load
User receives feedback on save success or failure

tasks.title: Builder view component tests
tasks.summary: Vitest + React Testing Library tests for the Builder view components.
tasks.description: Write tests in apps/desktop/src/__tests__/. Mock Tauri invoke(). Test: canvas renders a workflow definition, node palette creates nodes, node selection opens inspector, edge creation adds an edge to graph state, validation error display. Use the Tauri API mock pattern from TESTING.md.
acceptance_criteria: npm test passes for builder-related tests
All Tauri invoke() calls are mocked
Node creation, selection, and edge creation are tested
Validation error display is tested

tasks.title: Live run view layout and event subscription
tasks.summary: Implement the Live Run view shell with layout panels and Tauri event subscriptions.
tasks.description: Create the Live Run view with panels: animated graph, event feed, command output, and run HUD. Subscribe to Tauri events (via listen()) for all RunEvent types. Maintain a React state model of the current run derived from received events. The UI must not poll — events drive state.
acceptance_criteria: Live Run view is reachable from the app shell
Tauri event subscription is set up for all event families
Received events update React state
Run HUD shows run_id, workflow name, status, workspace_root, and elapsed time

tasks.title: Animated graph node states in live view
tasks.summary: Render the workflow graph in live view with per-node state badges and animations.
tasks.description: Render a read-only React Flow graph in the live view. Update each node's visual state (idle, queued, running, waiting, succeeded, failed, skipped, paused) as events arrive. Running nodes should show a subtle pulse animation. Waiting nodes show an amber hold state. Failed nodes show a high-contrast alert. Active edges show directional motion. Per DESIGN_SPEC.md §7.
acceptance_criteria: All 8 node states have distinct visual styles
Running nodes animate with a pulse
Failed nodes are visually prominent (high contrast)
Active edges show directional flow
Node states update correctly as events are received

tasks.title: Event feed panel
tasks.summary: Implement a scrolling event feed panel showing live events as they arrive.
tasks.description: Render a chronological event feed as events are received. Show event_type, timestamp, associated node label, and a summary of the payload. Auto-scroll to the latest event. Allow clicking an event to show full payload details. Color-code by event family.
acceptance_criteria: Events appear in the feed as they are received
Feed auto-scrolls to latest event
Clicking an event shows the full payload
Events are color-coded by family (run, node, command, review, etc.)

tasks.title: Command output panel
tasks.summary: Implement a terminal-like output panel showing stdout/stderr from the active node.
tasks.description: Show stdout and stderr streams from command events in a scrollable panel. Separate stdout and stderr visually if available. Show timestamps or event associations. Support copy/select. Allow collapse/expand for noisy output. Link output to the originating node. Per DESIGN_SPEC.md §9.
acceptance_criteria: Stdout and stderr from command events appear in the panel
Panel is within the app layout, not full-screen
Collapse/expand works for long outputs
Text is selectable and copyable
notes: DEC-004 asks whether to embed a terminal emulator component or build a custom output panel. Custom panel is the default.

tasks.title: Human review interaction in live run view
tasks.summary: Implement the human review UI that surfaces when the run is paused at a HumanReview node.
tasks.description: When a review.required event arrives, surface a modal or prominent panel showing: reason for review, relevant outputs and memory from the current run, and action buttons (Approve, Reject, Retry). Keyboard shortcuts for each action. Actions call submit_review_decision via Tauri. Per DESIGN_SPEC.md §10.
acceptance_criteria: review.required event triggers the review panel
Panel shows reason, outputs, and available actions
Approve/Reject/Retry buttons invoke the correct Tauri command
Keyboard shortcuts work for each action
Panel closes and run resumes/fails after the decision

tasks.title: Live run view component tests
tasks.summary: Vitest + React Testing Library tests for the Live Run view components.
tasks.description: Mock Tauri event subscriptions and invoke(). Test: node state badges render correctly for each NodeStatus, event feed populates from mocked events, human review panel appears on review.required, command output panel shows stdout/stderr content.
acceptance_criteria: npm test passes for live run tests
All 8 node state visuals are tested
Human review panel test covers all three actions
No real Tauri bridge calls in tests

tasks.title: Replay view layout and event loading
tasks.summary: Implement the Replay view shell that loads a completed run's event log for inspection.
tasks.description: Create the Replay view with panels: timeline scrubber, event list, graph playback, and event detail inspector. On entering replay view for a run, invoke list_events_for_run and load the event sequence into local state. All state in replay view is derived from the stored event sequence — never from current engine state.
acceptance_criteria: Replay view is accessible from the Library view
Events for the selected run are loaded via Tauri command
Event list renders all events in chronological order
Initial scrubber position is at the first event

tasks.title: Timeline scrubber component
tasks.summary: Implement a timeline scrubber that navigates the event sequence by position or event.
tasks.description: Implement a scrubber component that allows the user to move backward and forward through the event sequence. Scrubbing to a position derives node states at that moment by replaying events up to that point. Support keyboard-based scrubbing (arrow keys). Per DESIGN_SPEC.md §4.3.
acceptance_criteria: Scrubber moves forward/backward through events
Keyboard arrow keys scrub the timeline
Graph state updates to reflect the selected event position
Scrubber position is visually clear

tasks.title: Event inspector and diff panes
tasks.summary: Implement an event inspector showing full payload details and input/output diff panes for the selected event.
tasks.description: When an event is selected in the replay view, show the full event payload (formatted JSON), and for node.started/node.succeeded events, show input and output diffs or memory state changes. Support inspecting: routing decisions, command details, agent outputs, memory reads/writes, and review actions.
acceptance_criteria: Selected event shows full typed payload
Node-related events show input/output context
Routing events show branch_selected reason
Command events show command, exit_code, stdout/stderr summary

tasks.title: Replay view component tests
tasks.summary: Vitest + React Testing Library tests for the Replay view.
tasks.description: Mock list_events_for_run response with a fixed event sequence. Test: events load and populate the timeline, scrubber position changes update graph node states, clicking an event shows its payload in the inspector.
acceptance_criteria: npm test passes for replay tests
Scrubber position affects node states (tested with mocked event sequence)
Event inspector shows correct payload for the selected event

tasks.title: Library view
tasks.summary: Implement the Library view listing all workflows and recent run history.
tasks.description: Implement the Library view with: a workflow card list (name, version, updated_at, recent run status), run history per workflow (run_id, status, started_at, duration), import workflow button (load a workflow.json from disk), export workflow button (save current workflow.json to disk). Clicking a workflow opens the Builder. Clicking a run opens Replay.
acceptance_criteria: Library view lists all saved workflows
Each workflow shows recent run status
Clicking a run opens the Replay view for that run
Import/export workflow works correctly

tasks.title: Global keyboard navigation
tasks.summary: Implement keyboard shortcuts for all major actions across all views.
tasks.description: Implement keyboard-driven navigation per DESIGN_SPEC.md §13: (1) view switching shortcuts, (2) canvas navigation (arrow keys, zoom), (3) node selection and connection, (4) run start/stop/pause, (5) human review approve/reject, (6) timeline scrubbing in Replay. A power user should complete a full workflow — build, run, inspect, replay — primarily by keyboard.
acceptance_criteria: View switching is keyboard-accessible
Canvas can be navigated without a mouse
Run start/stop/pause have keyboard shortcuts
Human review actions have keyboard shortcuts
Replay timeline is keyboard-scrubable

tasks.title: Mission control visual theme
tasks.summary: Implement the mission control / strategy HUD visual design system.
tasks.description: Define a CSS design system in styles/ implementing the mission control aesthetic: dark background, grid overlays, restrained motion, state glows/rings, layered panel depth. Avoid cartoonish or over-saturated arcade aesthetic. Reference: lazygit, k9s, Insomnia. Implement motion guidelines from DESIGN_SPEC.md §11 (edge flow animation, node pulse, timeline playhead).
acceptance_criteria: App uses a consistent dark, mission-control visual theme
Running nodes have a subtle pulse animation
Active edges show directional flow
Motion is restrained — no constant animation everywhere
Text remains readable at all states

tasks.title: First-run experience and demo workflow onboarding
tasks.summary: On first launch, offer the demo workflow immediately without requiring the user to build anything.
tasks.description: On first launch (detect via settings), display an onboarding screen or auto-load the plan-execute-critique-approve workflow into the Library. Prompt the user to select a workspace and run the demo. The path from app launch to first run replay must be achievable in under 2 minutes. Per PRD.md §16.
acceptance_criteria: On first launch, the demo workflow is immediately available in the Library
User can select a workspace and start the demo run without building anything from scratch
The first run produces events that can be replayed
Onboarding does not require completing a tutorial
notes: This is a critical UX success criterion per DECISIONS.md.

tasks.title: Distribution artifacts: GitHub Releases and package managers
tasks.summary: Set up native OS installer builds for GitHub Releases and formulae for Homebrew, winget, and AUR.
tasks.description: Configure Tauri bundler to produce: .dmg (macOS), .msi (Windows), .AppImage (Linux). Set up a GitHub Actions release workflow that builds all three on tag push and attaches them to the GitHub Release. Create a Homebrew formula, a winget manifest, and an AUR PKGBUILD. Per PRD.md §16 and DECISIONS.md.
acceptance_criteria: Tagged release builds produce .dmg, .msi, and .AppImage artifacts
Artifacts are attached to the GitHub Release automatically
Homebrew formula installs the app on macOS
winget manifest installs the app on Windows
AUR PKGBUILD is functional on Arch Linux
App is NOT distributed via npm for the GUI
notes: Per DECISIONS.md: do not use npm for the GUI app. npm is only appropriate for a future headless CLI companion (out of v1 scope).

tasks.title: Keep REPO_STRUCTURE.md in sync with actual layout
tasks.summary: Update REPO_STRUCTURE.md to reflect the actual repository layout as implementation progresses.
tasks.description: As new files and directories are created, update docs/REPO_STRUCTURE.md. Specifically: add apps/desktop/src/views/, components/, hooks/, state/; add tests/e2e/; confirm crate module structures. Run this update after each major phase completes.
acceptance_criteria: REPO_STRUCTURE.md matches the actual directory layout
All new view, component, and hook directories are documented

tasks.title: Update EVENT_SCHEMA.md as typed event implementations settle
tasks.summary: Update EVENT_SCHEMA.md with final typed payload shapes once the event-model crate is implemented.
tasks.description: After EVENT-002 through EVENT-007 are complete, review EVENT_SCHEMA.md and ensure all payload examples match the actual Rust struct fields. Add examples for any events not currently documented with payload shapes.
acceptance_criteria: EVENT_SCHEMA.md payload examples match implemented Rust structs
Every event type has at least a basic payload example

tasks.title: Update DECISIONS.md as open decisions are resolved
tasks.summary: Move resolved decisions from Open to Accepted in DECISIONS.md and add rationale.
tasks.description: As each DEC-00x task is resolved, add the decision to the Accepted section of DECISIONS.md with context, rationale, tradeoffs, and follow-up implications. Per AGENTS.md §14 ADR policy.
acceptance_criteria: All resolved DEC tasks have a corresponding accepted decision entry
Each entry includes context, decision, alternatives considered, and tradeoffs

tasks.title: E2E test setup: tauri-driver + WebdriverIO
tasks.summary: Set up the E2E test infrastructure using tauri-driver and WebdriverIO.
tasks.description: Configure tests/e2e/wdio.conf.js (file exists as skeleton) to point at the compiled debug binary. Install WebdriverIO and the Tauri service. Document setup steps for running tauri-driver + WebdriverIO locally and in CI. Set up a dedicated test workspace directory for E2E runs.
acceptance_criteria: tauri-driver is installed and can be started
WebdriverIO connects to the running app
A trivial test (app loads, title is visible) passes
Setup instructions are documented in docs/TESTING.md or a README

tasks.title: E2E test: builder flow
tasks.summary: E2E test covering the full builder flow: open app, create nodes, connect edges, save workflow.
tasks.description: Write a WebdriverIO spec that: opens the app, navigates to Builder view, adds nodes of each type from the palette, draws edges between them, saves the workflow, and verifies it appears in the Library view.
acceptance_criteria: E2E test creates a workflow with all 7 node types
Edges are created and saved
Saved workflow appears in Library view
Test passes reliably against a compiled Tauri build

tasks.title: E2E test: run flow and node state transitions
tasks.summary: E2E test loading the demo workflow, starting a run, and verifying node state transitions.
tasks.description: Write a WebdriverIO spec that: loads the plan-execute-critique-approve workflow, selects a test workspace, starts a run, and asserts that each node transitions through correct states (queued, running, waiting/succeeded). Use a dedicated test workspace — never a real project.
acceptance_criteria: Demo workflow loads and run starts
Node states are observed transitioning via the live run view
Run reaches the HumanReview node and pauses
Test uses a dedicated test workspace, not a real project

tasks.title: E2E test: human review gate
tasks.summary: E2E test verifying that a HumanReview node pauses the run and approve resumes it.
tasks.description: Write a WebdriverIO spec that runs the demo workflow to the HumanReview node, verifies the review panel appears, clicks Approve, and verifies the run continues to completion.
acceptance_criteria: Run pauses at the HumanReview node
Review panel is visible with the correct reason
Clicking Approve resumes the run
Run reaches the End node and transitions to Succeeded

tasks.title: E2E test: replay flow
tasks.summary: E2E test opening a completed run in Replay view and scrubbing the timeline.
tasks.description: Write a WebdriverIO spec that: opens a completed run from the Library, navigates to the Replay view, scrubs the timeline forward and backward, and verifies that node states update to reflect the selected position.
acceptance_criteria: Replay view opens a completed run
Timeline scrubbing changes node states on the graph
Event inspector shows correct payload for the selected event

tasks.title: E2E test: failure handling
tasks.summary: E2E test running a workflow where a Tool node fails and verifying Failed state is shown.
tasks.description: Write a WebdriverIO spec using a workflow where the Tool node runs a command that exits with a non-zero code. Verify: the node transitions to Failed state, the run transitions to Failed, the failed node is visually prominent in the live view.
acceptance_criteria: Tool node with failing command shows Failed state
Run transitions to Failed
Failed node is visually distinct (high contrast per DESIGN_SPEC.md)

tasks.title: Decide: workflow JSON schema versioning strategy
tasks.summary: Resolve how workflow JSON files are versioned and how forward/backward compatibility is handled.
tasks.description: The current WorkflowDefinition has a version field (u32) but no migration strategy. Decide: (1) is the version field a schema version or a user-controlled revision number? (2) How are older schema versions migrated on load? (3) What is the canonical version bump policy? Record the decision in DECISIONS.md.
acceptance_criteria: Decision is recorded in DECISIONS.md with context, alternatives, and tradeoffs
Versioning behavior is implemented consistently in schemas/workflow.schema.json and the Rust model
A migration path exists for at least one version bump scenario
notes: This blocks FOUND-003 and MODEL-005 from being considered fully stable.

tasks.title: Decide: bounded loop edges in v1 or defer to v1.1
tasks.summary: Decide whether to support bounded loop edges in the v1 graph or defer entirely.
tasks.description: The ConditionKind enum currently has always, on_success, on_failure, expression. Bounded loops would require a loop edge kind with a counter or condition limit. Decide: include in v1 (adds complexity to scheduler and guardrails) or defer (simpler v1, add in v1.1). Per AGENTS.md Rule E.
acceptance_criteria: Decision is recorded in DECISIONS.md
If deferred: ConditionKind is stable without loop kind
If included: loop edge semantics are fully specified before implementation
notes: Default recommendation: defer to v1.1. Bounded loops add significant scheduler complexity.

tasks.title: Decide: cross-platform changed file detection
tasks.summary: Decide how to detect files changed by a CLI command reliably across macOS, Windows, and Linux.
tasks.description: When a Tool or Agent node runs a command, the engine should ideally detect changed files and log them as metadata. Options: (1) git diff before/after (requires workspace to be a git repo), (2) file system watcher during execution, (3) no detection in v1 (log command metadata only). Decide and document.
acceptance_criteria: Decision is recorded in DECISIONS.md
If detecting: implementation approach is specified for all three platforms
If not detecting in v1: this is noted as a deferred enhancement

tasks.title: Decide: terminal emulator component vs custom output panel
tasks.summary: Decide whether to embed a full terminal emulator component or build a custom styled output panel.
tasks.description: Options: (1) embed an existing terminal emulator (e.g. xterm.js) for full ANSI/VT100 support, (2) build a custom styled div-based output panel with stdout/stderr separation and event association. Custom panel is simpler; xterm.js is better for ANSI-heavy tools. DESIGN_SPEC.md §9 specifies the panel should be within the app, not full-screen.
acceptance_criteria: Decision is recorded in DECISIONS.md
If xterm.js: package is added to package.json and integration path documented
If custom panel: ANSI color stripping/rendering approach is specified
notes: This decision unblocks UI-RUN-004.

tasks.title: Decide: structured output requirements for agent CLI nodes
tasks.summary: Decide how much structured output is required from CLI-backed agent nodes and how it is extracted.
tasks.description: Agent nodes produce output that downstream nodes (via memory) need to consume. Options: (1) require structured JSON on stdout from the agent CLI, (2) capture raw text and let downstream nodes use it as-is, (3) use a configurable output parsing strategy per node. This affects AgentNodeConfig design and ADAPT-003 implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Decision is recorded in DECISIONS.md
The chosen approach is reflected in MODEL-005 (AgentNodeConfig)
ADAPT-003 implementation aligns with the decision
notes: This decision unblocks MODEL-005 and ADAPT-003.

tasks.title: Tauri IPC boundary contract
tasks.summary: Define and document every Tauri invoke() command and listen() event payload before any run-lifecycle or event-bridge implementation begins.
tasks.description:
Create docs/TAURI_IPC_CONTRACT.md that specifies:
1. Every invoke() command name, its exact Rust argument struct (as TypeScript interface), and return type.
2. Every listen() event name emitted by the backend, its exact payload shape, and which frontend component subscribes.
3. Error variant shapes returned from each command (Tauri serialises Rust Result::Err as a string or typed object — document which).

The contract must cover at minimum:
- create_run / start_run / cancel_run / get_run / list_runs_for_workflow
- get_run_events (for replay)
- submit_human_review_decision
- Events: run_status_changed, node_status_changed, run_event_appended, human_review_requested

The TypeScript interfaces defined here become the canonical source for UI-RUN, UI-RPL, and TAURI-002/003/004 implementation. Any deviation between Rust impl and this contract is a bug, not a design choice.

Also create apps/desktop/src/types/ipc.ts mirroring every interface from the contract so TypeScript consumers have a typed invoke wrapper rather than casting unknown at every call site.
acceptance_criteria: docs/TAURI_IPC_CONTRACT.md exists and covers all run-lifecycle and event-bridge commands/events
apps/desktop/src/types/ipc.ts exports TypeScript interfaces for every invoke arg, return, and event payload
Each command entry includes: command name, Rust struct equivalent, return type, error type, and example JSON
Each event entry includes: event name, payload interface, emitter (Rust function), and subscriber (React component/hook)
TAURI-002, TAURI-003, TAURI-004 implement against this contract — no undocumented commands or events
A utility invokeTyped<T>() wrapper in ipc.ts that calls invoke() with the typed arg and casts the result
notes: BLOCKS: TAURI-002, TAURI-003, TAURI-004 — do not implement those until this contract is approved
Tauri 2.x serialises Result<T, E> where E: ToString as a plain string error. Document this clearly.
The invokeTyped wrapper prevents silent any-casts accumulating across the UI codebase

tasks.title: Engine test-first mandate: validator and coordinator
tasks.summary: Write failing unit tests for ENGINE-003 (workflow validator) and ENGINE-004 (run coordinator) before implementing those tasks. Tests become the executable spec.
tasks.description:
Before ENGINE-003 and ENGINE-004 are coded, write a comprehensive unit test file for each:

crates/core-engine/src/validator_tests.rs — covering WorkflowValidator:
- valid minimal workflow passes (single start, single end, connected)
- workflow with no start node fails with ValidationError::NoStartNode
- workflow with no end node fails with ValidationError::NoEndNode
- workflow with multiple start nodes fails
- workflow with a cycle fails with ValidationError::CycleDetected
- workflow with an edge referencing a non-existent node fails
- workflow with an unreachable node fails or warns (decide and document in DECISIONS.md)
- all v1 NodeKind variants are accepted by the validator

crates/core-engine/src/coordinator_tests.rs — covering RunCoordinator:
- new run starts in Created state
- valid run transitions Created -> Validating -> Ready -> Running
- invalid transition (e.g. Running -> Created) returns StateTransitionError
- cancelling a running run transitions to Cancelled and emits RunCancelled event
- a node completing with success transitions its state to Succeeded and queues next nodes
- a node failing with no retries left transitions to Failed and the run to Failed
- pausing on a HumanReview node transitions the run to Paused and emits HumanReviewRequested
- approving a paused run resumes and transitions correctly

Tests must compile (but are expected to FAIL until ENGINE-003/004 implement the code). Use an in-memory event log stub (not SQLite) for coordinator tests to keep them fast and isolated.
acceptance_criteria: crates/core-engine/src/validator_tests.rs exists with >= 8 named test cases for WorkflowValidator
crates/core-engine/src/coordinator_tests.rs exists with >= 8 named test cases for RunCoordinator
All test cases are #[test] annotated and compile cleanly (cargo check -p core-engine passes)
Tests are expected to fail at this stage — that is correct; they define the contract ENGINE-003/004 must satisfy
ValidationError enum variants are declared (even if not implemented) so tests can reference them
RunCoordinator accepts an injected event log trait object so tests can use an in-memory stub
All test names follow the pattern: fn <verb>_<condition>_<expected_outcome>()
DECISIONS.md updated with the unreachable-node policy (error vs warning)
notes: BLOCKS: ENGINE-003, ENGINE-004 — those tasks must make these tests pass
Use trait injection (not direct SQLite) for the event log in coordinator tests
The ValidationError and StateTransitionError types defined here become the canonical error vocabulary for the engine
Coordinate with QA-001 — event names emitted in coordinator tests must match the IPC contract

tasks.title: App: pass onOpenLiveRun callback to LibraryView
tasks.summary: Wire the existing openLiveRun function in App.tsx into LibraryView so a run can be linked to the LiveRunView via the UI.
tasks.description: App.tsx already defines openLiveRun(runId) which sets liveRunId state and switches to the liverun view. LibraryView currently receives onOpenReplay and onOpenBuilder but not onOpenLiveRun. Add the onOpenLiveRun?: (runId: string) => void prop to LibraryView and pass openLiveRun from App.tsx. No behaviour changes to other views.
acceptance_criteria: LibraryView accepts an onOpenLiveRun?: (runId: string) => void prop
App.tsx passes openLiveRun as onOpenLiveRun to LibraryView
Calling onOpenLiveRun(runId) in LibraryView navigates to the Live Run view with that run ID
No regression in existing LibraryView behaviour (replay, builder, import/export)
notes: This is the missing navigation link identified during TEST-003 implementation.

tasks.title: LibraryView: Start Run button with workspace picker
tasks.summary: Add a Start Run button to each workflow card in LibraryView. Clicking it prompts for a workspace directory, calls create_run + start_run via IPC, then navigates to the Live Run view.
tasks.description: Each workflow card in LibraryView needs a Start Run button. Clicking it: (1) shows an inline workspace path input (or uses the last-used workspace stored in component state), (2) calls ipc.createRun({ workflow_id, workspace_root }) then ipc.startRun({ run_id }), (3) calls onOpenLiveRun(run.run_id) to navigate to the Live Run view with the new run. Error handling: show an inline error if createRun or startRun fails. The workspace input must default to an empty string — never auto-fill a real project path. Use data-testid attributes so E2E tests can locate the button and input.
acceptance_criteria: Each workflow card has a Start Run button ([data-testid="start-run-{workflowId}"])
Clicking Start Run shows a workspace path input ([data-testid="workspace-input-{workflowId}"])
Submitting the form calls create_run and start_run via ipc.*
After start_run succeeds, onOpenLiveRun(runId) is called and Live Run view opens
Errors from IPC are displayed inline next to the button
Workspace input defaults to empty string (never a real project path)
notes: data-testid values needed by TEST-004: start-run-{workflowId}, workspace-input-{workflowId}
